content.problemStatement:
There are two people walking along a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN), each of them with speed 1 edge per second.

The walk of the first person is described by a sequence of nodes u1,u2,…,uKu_1, u_2, \dots, u_Ku1​,u2​,…,uK​. This person appears in node u1u_1u1​ at time 000 and walks to node u2u_2u2​ using the shortest **path**; then, immediately after reaching u2u_2u2​, walks to node u3u_3u3​ using the shortest path, and so on, until reaching uKu_KuK​, when this person disappears.

The walk of the second person is described by another sequence v1,v2,…,vLv_1, v_2, \dots, v_Lv1​,v2​,…,vL​ in the same manner.

Count how many times these two people meet — in other words, how many times they reach the same node (including the first and last nodes of their walks) at the same time.
constraints: 1 ≤ ui, vi ≤ N for each valid i
ui ≠ ui+1 for each valid i
vi ≠ vi+1 for each valid i
The sum of N in all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
The sum of K in all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
The sum of L in all test cases does not exceed 5*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, K, and L. Each of the next N-1 lines contains two space-separated integers p and q describing an edge between node p and node q. The following line contains K space-separated integers u1, u2, …, uK. The last line contains L space-separated integers v1, v2, …, vL.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of times the two people meet.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The two people meet at nodes 1, 3, and 5.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph traversal approach. We first construct the graph using the given edges. Then, for each pair of nodes in the two sequences, we find the shortest path between them using BFS. We keep track of the time it takes for each person to reach each node and count the number of times they meet at the same node at the same time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*(N+K+L)*N) — the time complexity is dominated by the BFS traversal for each pair of nodes in the sequences.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N+K+L) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the graph and the sequences.

code.Python3:
import sys
from collections import deque

MAXN = 100005
MAXK = 100005
MAXL = 100005
INF = 0x3f3f3f3f

n, k, l = 0, 0, 0
u = [0] * MAXK
v = [0] * MAXL
dist = [[INF] * MAXN for _ in range(MAXN)]
graph = [[] for _ in range(MAXN)]

def bfs(start, dist, graph):
    q = deque([start])
    dist[start] = 0
    while q:
        node = q.popleft()
        for neighbor in graph[node]:
            if dist[neighbor] == INF:
                q.append(neighbor)
                dist[neighbor] = dist[node] + 1

for _ in range(int(input())):
    n, k, l = map(int, input().split())
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        graph[i].clear()
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            dist[i][j] = INF
    for _ in range(n - 1):
        p, q = map(int, input().split())
        graph[p].append(q)
        graph[q].append(p)
    u[1:] = map(int, input().split())
    v[1:] = map(int, input().split())
    count = 0
    for i in range(1, k + 1):
        for j in range(1, l + 1):
            bfs(u[i], dist[u[i]], graph)
            bfs(v[j], dist[v[j]], graph)
            if dist[u[i]][v[j]] == dist[v[j]][u[i]]:
                count += 1
    print(count)
